statement.p: A certain country has 451.5 million acres of forest. Every year, the country loses 6.45 million acres of forest mainly due to deforestation for farming purposes. If this situation continues at this pace, how many years later will the country have only 251.55 million acres of forest left? (Use an equation to solve this problem.)
After years, this country would have 251.55 million acres of forest left.

solution.p: Assume after y years, this country will have 251.55 million acres of forest left.
The country loses 6.45 million acres of forest every year. In y years, the country will lose a total of 6.45y million acres of forest.
Currently, the country has 451.5 million acres of forest. In y years, with a loss of 6.45y million acres, there would be 451.5-6.45y million acres of forest left.
The question asks when there would be 251.55 million acres of forest left. Now we can write an equation:
After {31} years, this country would have 251.55 million acres of forest left.
